texts.orig: WHEREAS Mawili Nganda (deceased), is registered as proprietor of all that piece of land known as Okia/Mukuyuni/1749, situate in the district  of  Makueni,  and  whereas  in  the  Chief  Magistrate's  Court  in Succession  Cause  No.  E216  of  2024,  has  issued  grant  of  letters  of administration  and  certificate  of  confirmation  of  grant  in  favour  of Peter  Mwau  Ndaya,  and  whereas  the  said  court  has  executed  an application to be registered as proprietor by transmission of R.L. 19, and whereas the title deed in in respect of Mawili Nganda (deceased) is lost, notice is given that after the expiration of thirty (30) days from the date hereof, provided no valid objection has been received within that  period,  I  intend  to  dispense  with  the  production  of  the  said  land title  deed  and  proceed  with  registration  of  the  said  application  to  be registered as proprietor by transmission R.L. 19 in the name of Peter Mwau  Ndaya,  and  upon  such  registration  the  land  title  deed  issued earlier  to  the  said  Mawili  Nganda  (deceased),  shall  be  deemed  to  be cancelled and of no effect.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: WHEREAS William Kiptanui Chemweno (deceased), is registered as  proprietor  of  all  that  piece  of  land  known  as  Irong/Kapkonga/1, situate  in  the  district  of  Elgeiyo/Marakwet,  and  whereas  in  the  High Court of Kenya at Eldoret in Succession Cause No. E6 of 2021, has issued grant of letters of administration and certificate of confirmation of grant in favour of (1) Wilson Kiplangat Kalya, (2) John Kiplangat Chemweno, (3) Ambrose Kipkorir Chemweno and (4) Peter Kipngetich Sawe,  and whereas the said court has executed an application  to  be  registered  as  proprietor  by  transmission  of  R.L.  39 and R.L. 42 in respect to the said piece of land registered in the name of William Kiptanui Chemweno (deceased), and whereas the land title deed issued is missing or lost, notice is given that after the expiration of  sixty  (60)  days  from  the  date  hereof,  provided  no  valid  objection has  been  received  within  that  period,  I  intend  to  dispense  with  the production of the said land title deed, and proceed with the registration of R.L. 39 and R.L. 42, and upon such registration the land title deed issued  earlier  to  William  Kiptanui  Chemweno  (deceased),  shall  be deemed to be cancelled and of no effect.

texts.orig: KENOL-SAGANA-MARUA ROAD PROJECT DELETION AND ADDENDUM
texts.text: KENOL-SAGANA-MARUA ROAD PROJECT DELETION AND ADDENDUM

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN PURSUANCE of the Land Act 2012, Part VIII and further to Gazette Notice Nos. 7661 of 2020, 1702, 4389, 5409, 7894 and 7895 of 2021, the National Land Commission on behalf of Kenya National Highways  Authority  gives notice that the  National  Government intends  to delete and add the  following  parcel  of  land  required  for construction  of  Kenol-Sagana-Marua  Road  Project  in  Nyeri  and Kirinyaga Counties.
texts.text: IN PURSUANCE of the Land Act 2012, Part VIII and further to Gazette Notice Nos. 7661 of 2020, 1702, 4389, 5409, 7894 and 7895 of 2021, the National Land Commission on behalf of Kenya National Highways  Authority  gives notice that the  National  Government intends  to delete and add the  following  parcel  of  land  required  for construction  of  Kenol-Sagana-Marua  Road  Project  in  Nyeri  and Kirinyaga Counties.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Plan for the affected land may be inspected during office hours at the office of the National Land Commission, Ardhi House, 3rd Floor, Room  305,  1st  Ngong  Avenue,  Nairobi  and  at  the  National  Land Commission's  County  Co-ordinator's  Office  in  Nyeri  and  Kirinyaga Counties.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: RESERVATION OF PUBLIC LAND TO KENYA WILDLIFE SERVICE
texts.text: RESERVATION OF PUBLIC LAND TO KENYA WILDLIFE SERVICE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control, and management of the land parcel L.R. No. 30446, containing approximately  4.46  hectares  or  thereabout,  and  situated  in  Kajiado County,  as  described  in  FR  568/96,  to  Kenya  Wildlife  Service, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.
texts.text: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control, and management of the land parcel L.R. No. 30446, containing approximately  4.46  hectares  or  thereabout,  and  situated  in  Kajiado County,  as  described  in  FR  568/96,  to  Kenya  Wildlife  Service, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions;
texts.text: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. The land shall only be used as staff quarters.
texts.text: The land shall only be used as staff quarters.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.
texts.text: The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. The Management Body shall within 180 days of publishing of this reservation order cause to be prepared and submit to the National Land Commission for approval a plan for the development management and use of the reserved land parcels failure to which the management orders will stand revoked.
texts.text: The Management Body shall within 180 days of publishing of this reservation order cause to be prepared and submit to the National Land Commission for approval a plan for the development management and use of the reserved land parcels failure to which the management orders will stand revoked.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. The preparation of the said development plan shall-
texts.text: The preparation of the said development plan shall-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;
texts.text: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) Consider any conservation, environmental or heritage issues relevant to the development, management or use of the public land in its managed reserve;
texts.text: (b) Consider any conservation, environmental or heritage issues relevant to the development, management or use of the public land in its managed reserve;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) Comply with the values and principles of the Constitution;
texts.text: (c) Comply with the values and principles of the Constitution;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) Include a statement that it has taken into consideration the above issues in drawing the plan.
texts.text: (d) Include a statement that it has taken into consideration the above issues in drawing the plan.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. The Management Body shall submit an environmental impact assessment plan pursuant to existing law on environment.
texts.text: The Management Body shall submit an environmental impact assessment plan pursuant to existing law on environment.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. The Management Body ensure that at least 10% of the land is covered by trees and vegetation as part of the Constitutional requirement towards achievement of at least 10% tree cover and green growth agenda.
texts.text: The Management Body ensure that at least 10% of the land is covered by trees and vegetation as part of the Constitutional requirement towards achievement of at least 10% tree cover and green growth agenda.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7. The  Management  Body  shall  comply  with  the  ideals  and principles  governing  circular  economy  including  waste  elimination, refusal, recycling, re-use, remanufacture and composting.
texts.text: The  Management  Body  shall  comply  with  the  ideals  and principles  governing  circular  economy  including  waste  elimination, refusal, recycling, re-use, remanufacture and composting.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8. The Management Body shall support and adopt systems and mechanisms for nature regeneration and sustainable landscape management.
texts.text: The Management Body shall support and adopt systems and mechanisms for nature regeneration and sustainable landscape management.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9. The Management Body shall adopt climate smart technologies for operations and processes that reduce carbon footprints and emissions.
texts.text: The Management Body shall adopt climate smart technologies for operations and processes that reduce carbon footprints and emissions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10. The  Management Body shall  adhere  to  the  Global  Industry Practices  (GIPs)  that  promote  good  environmental  and  public  health standards.
texts.text: The  Management Body shall  adhere  to  the  Global  Industry Practices  (GIPs)  that  promote  good  environmental  and  public  health standards.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11. The  Management  Body  shall  ensure  proper  documentation, stock-take,  monitoring  and  reporting  of  the  land-scape  level  critical biodiversity resources including measures taken towards their protection and conservation.
texts.text: The  Management  Body  shall  ensure  proper  documentation, stock-take,  monitoring  and  reporting  of  the  land-scape  level  critical biodiversity resources including measures taken towards their protection and conservation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12. The  Management  Body  shall  pay  such  rates,  ground  rent, duties, assessments or outgoings payable to the National Government and County Government from time to time.
texts.text: The  Management  Body  shall  pay  such  rates,  ground  rent, duties, assessments or outgoings payable to the National Government and County Government from time to time.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 13. The Management Body shall facilitate the harmonious coordination  of  services  provided  by  other  public  agencies  on  the reserved land, ensuring optimal and efficient utilization.
texts.text: The Management Body shall facilitate the harmonious coordination  of  services  provided  by  other  public  agencies  on  the reserved land, ensuring optimal and efficient utilization.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 14. The Management Body shall be responsible for safeguarding the land making sure the demarcated boundaries of the land parcels are well maintained.
texts.text: The Management Body shall be responsible for safeguarding the land making sure the demarcated boundaries of the land parcels are well maintained.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: RESERVATION OF PUBLIC LAND TO THE STATE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENCE
texts.text: RESERVATION OF PUBLIC LAND TO THE STATE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENCE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, and section 105 (2) of the Land Registration Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control and management of the land parcel L.R. No. 33517, containing approximately 1010.07 hectares or thereabout,  and  situated  in  Turkana  County,  as  described  in  FR  No. 537/45 to State Department of Defence, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.
texts.text: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, and section 105 (2) of the Land Registration Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control and management of the land parcel L.R. No. 33517, containing approximately 1010.07 hectares or thereabout,  and  situated  in  Turkana  County,  as  described  in  FR  No. 537/45 to State Department of Defence, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions:
texts.text: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. The land shall only be used as a Military Camp.
texts.text: The land shall only be used as a Military Camp.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.
texts.text: The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. The  Management  Body  shall  within  one  hundred  and  eighty (180) days of publishing of this reservation order cause to be prepared and submit to the National  Land  Commission  a  land  use  and management plan of the reserved land parcel.
texts.text: The  Management  Body  shall  within  one  hundred  and  eighty (180) days of publishing of this reservation order cause to be prepared and submit to the National  Land  Commission  a  land  use  and management plan of the reserved land parcel.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. The preparation of the plan referred in (3) shall-
texts.text: The preparation of the plan referred in (3) shall-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;
texts.text: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) Consider any conservation, environmental or heritage issues relevant to the development, management or use of the public land in its managed reserve;
texts.text: (b) Consider any conservation, environmental or heritage issues relevant to the development, management or use of the public land in its managed reserve;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) Comply with the values and principles of the Constitution;
texts.text: (c) Comply with the values and principles of the Constitution;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) Allow  for  controlled/regulated  access  to  critical  areas  for community  livelihoods  and  resilience  including  grazing, salt licks and livestock watering points;
texts.text: (d) Allow  for  controlled/regulated  access  to  critical  areas  for community  livelihoods  and  resilience  including  grazing, salt licks and livestock watering points;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) Provide  access  to  critical  areas  and  pathways  for  wildlife movement  (corridors) and dispersal areas in order to mitigate against potential human-wildlife conflicts.
texts.text: (e) Provide  access  to  critical  areas  and  pathways  for  wildlife movement  (corridors) and dispersal areas in order to mitigate against potential human-wildlife conflicts.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. The Management Body shall prepare and submit an environmental and social impact assessment report before any major land  development  projects,  to  identify  potential  environmental  and social risks and propose mitigation strategies in line with the Environmental Management and Co-ordination Act, 1999 (EMCA).
texts.text: The Management Body shall prepare and submit an environmental and social impact assessment report before any major land  development  projects,  to  identify  potential  environmental  and social risks and propose mitigation strategies in line with the Environmental Management and Co-ordination Act, 1999 (EMCA).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. The Management Body ensure that at least 10% of the land is covered by trees and vegetation as part of the Constitutional requirement towards achievement of at least 10% tree cover and green growth agenda.
texts.text: The Management Body ensure that at least 10% of the land is covered by trees and vegetation as part of the Constitutional requirement towards achievement of at least 10% tree cover and green growth agenda.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7. texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, and section 105 (2) of the Land Registration Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control and management of the land parcel L.R.  No.  33518,  containing  approximately  1063.54  hectares  and situated  in  Turkana  County,  as  described  in  FR  NO.  537/46 to  State Department of Defence, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions:
texts.text: The Management Body shall hold and use the reserved land parcel subject to the following special conditions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. The land shall only be used as a Military Camp.
texts.text: The land shall only be used as a Military Camp.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.
texts.text: The Management Body shall not part with the possession of the reserved land parcels or any part thereof without the written consent of the National Land Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. The  Management  Body  shall  within  one  hundred  and  eighty (180) days of publishing of this reservation order cause to be prepared and submit to the National  Land  Commission  a  land  use  and management plan of the reserved land parcel.

texts.orig: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;
texts.text: (a) Take  into  consideration  the  physical  planning  regulations and other relevant laws in force;

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) Comply with the values and principles of the Constitution;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) Allow  for  controlled/regulated  access  to  critical  areas  for community  livelihoods  and  resilience  including  grazing, salt licks and livestock watering points;

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 15, 16, 17, and 18 of the Land Act, 2012, and section 105 (2) of the Land Registration Act, 2012, the National Land Commission issues this reservation order to reserve and vest the care, control and management of the land parcel L.R. NO. 33522, containing approximately 988.2 hectares and situated in Turkana  County,  as described in FR  NO.  690/124  to  State Department of Defence, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.

NO.  33527,  containing  approximately  1001.52  hectares  and situated in Turkana County, as described in FR NO. 537/144 to State Department of Defence, hereinafter referred to as 'The Management Body'.

texts.text: DECISIONS OF THE KENYA CIVIL AVIATION AUTHORITY ON APPLICATIONS FOR AIR SERVICE LICENCES

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PURSUANT to the provisions of the Civil Aviation Act, 2013 as read together with Civil Aviation (Amendment Act, 2016 and the Civil Aviation (Licensing  of  Air  Services)  Regulations,  2018  (Regulation  28),  notice  is  given  that  the  Kenya  Civil  Aviation  Authority  has  made  decisions  on applications for air service licences whose particulars were previously published in the Kenya Gazette Notices No. 16415 of 2024 and No. 3545 of 2025.
texts.text: PURSUANT to the provisions of the Civil Aviation Act, 2013 as read together with Civil Aviation (Amendment Act, 2016 and the Civil Aviation (Licensing  of  Air  Services)  Regulations,  2018  (Regulation  28),  notice  is  given  that  the  Kenya  Civil  Aviation  Authority  has  made  decisions  on applications for air service licences whose particulars were previously published in the Kenya Gazette Notices No. 16415 of 2024 and No. 3545 of 2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Decisions are specified in the third column and the particulars of the applications are in the second column for each applicant named in the first column of the Schedule below.

texts.orig: CALL FOR COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT ENERGY (BIOFUELS) REGULATIONS, 2025 AND DRAFT REGULATORY IMPACT STATEMENT
texts.text: CALL FOR COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT ENERGY (BIOFUELS) REGULATIONS, 2025 AND DRAFT REGULATORY IMPACT STATEMENT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (the Authority) is Kenya's energy sector regulatory agency established under the Energy Act, 2019, and is responsible for the technical and economic regulation of  the  Electricity,  Renewable  Energy  and  Petroleum  subsectors  in Kenya.  The  Authority  has  developed  the  Draft  Energy  (Biofuels) Regulations,  2025,  together  with  the  associated  Draft  Regulatory Impact  Statement.  The  purpose  of  the  regulations  is  to  promote  the adoption and use of biofuels by enforcing standards and ensuring fair business practice in the biofuels value chain in Kenya.
texts.text: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (the Authority) is Kenya's energy sector regulatory agency established under the Energy Act, 2019, and is responsible for the technical and economic regulation of  the  Electricity,  Renewable  Energy  and  Petroleum  subsectors  in Kenya.  The  Authority  has  developed  the  Draft  Energy  (Biofuels) Regulations,  2025,  together  with  the  associated  Draft  Regulatory Impact  Statement.  The  purpose  of  the  regulations  is  to  promote  the adoption and use of biofuels by enforcing standards and ensuring fair business practice in the biofuels value chain in Kenya.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Stakeholders are requested to review the aforementioned documents and provide feedback that will be considered for incorporation in the regulations that will be gazetted.
texts.text: Stakeholders are requested to review the aforementioned documents and provide feedback that will be considered for incorporation in the regulations that will be gazetted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Your written comments on the Draft Energy (Biofuels) Regulations, 2025 and the draft Regulatory Impact Statement using the prescribed public comments form on the Authority's website, may be submitted to reach the undersigned not later than thirty (30) days from the date of publication  of  this  notice.  The  memoranda  marked 'PUBLIC COMMENTS ON DRAFT BIOFUELS REGULATIONS' can  be  hand  delivered  to  the  Authority  offices  or  sent  by  email  to renewableenergy@epra.go.ke.
texts.text: Your written comments on the Draft Energy (Biofuels) Regulations, 2025 and the draft Regulatory Impact Statement using the prescribed public comments form on the Authority's website, may be submitted to reach the undersigned not later than thirty (30) days from the date of publication  of  this  notice.  The  memoranda  marked 'PUBLIC COMMENTS ON DRAFT BIOFUELS REGULATIONS' can  be  hand  delivered  to  the  Authority  offices  or  sent  by  email  to renewableenergy@epra.go.ke.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After the expiry of the thirty (30) day period for the submission of written memoranda, the Authority shall organize stakeholder consultation  workshops.  To  express  interest  to  participate  in  the consultative  meetings,  email:-  renewableenergy@epra.go.ke  or  call: 0709336000.  Details  of  the  meetings  shall  also  be  available  on  the Authority's website.
texts.text: After the expiry of the thirty (30) day period for the submission of written memoranda, the Authority shall organize stakeholder consultation  workshops.  To  express  interest  to  participate  in  the consultative  meetings,  email:-  renewableenergy@epra.go.ke  or  call: 0709336000.  Details  of  the  meetings  shall  also  be  available  on  the Authority's website.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A copy of the draft  Regulatory  Impact  Statement  is  available  on the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority's website www.epra.go.ke

texts.orig: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 75 and 208 of the Energy Act, 2019, the Cabinet Secretary makes the following Regulations:-
texts.text: IN EXERCISE of the powers conferred by sections 75 and 208 of the Energy Act, 2019, the Cabinet Secretary makes the following Regulations:-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. These regulations may  be cited as the Energy (Biofuels) Regulations, 2025.
texts.text: These regulations may  be cited as the Energy (Biofuels) Regulations, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires -
texts.text: In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires -

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 'Authority' has the meaning assigned to it under the Act.
texts.text: 'Authority' has the meaning assigned to it under the Act.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'biofuel' means bioethanol, biodiesel or biogas, produced through biological, and/or chemical conversion of organic matter for industrial heating applications, cooking, transport and electricity generation.
texts.text: 'biofuel' means bioethanol, biodiesel or biogas, produced through biological, and/or chemical conversion of organic matter for industrial heating applications, cooking, transport and electricity generation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'blending'  means  the  mixing  of  biofuels  with  a  petroleum product.
texts.text: 'blending'  means  the  mixing  of  biofuels  with  a  petroleum product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'distribution' means  the supply of biofuels through piped networks,  tankers,  barrels  or  such  other  authorised  means  from the producer to third parties or end consumers.
texts.text: 'distribution' means  the supply of biofuels through piped networks,  tankers,  barrels  or  such  other  authorised  means  from the producer to third parties or end consumers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'Kenya  Standard'  means  a  specification  or  code  of  practice declared by the Council under the Standards Act.
texts.text: 'Kenya  Standard'  means  a  specification  or  code  of  practice declared by the Council under the Standards Act.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'licensee'  means  the  holder  of  a  licence  issued  under  these Regulations;
texts.text: 'licensee'  means  the  holder  of  a  licence  issued  under  these Regulations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. These  regulations  shall  apply  to  the  production,  importation, exportation, transportation, storage, packaging, blending, distribution and sale of biofuels.
texts.text: These  regulations  shall  apply  to  the  production,  importation, exportation, transportation, storage, packaging, blending, distribution and sale of biofuels.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. The purpose of these regulations is to promote the adoption and use of biofuels by enforcing standards and ensuring fair business practice in the biofuels value chain.
texts.text: The purpose of these regulations is to promote the adoption and use of biofuels by enforcing standards and ensuring fair business practice in the biofuels value chain.

texts.label: text

texts.text: (w) A  person  who,  without  a  permit  or  licence  issued  by  the Authority,  undertakes  the  construction  of  a  biofuel facility, production, importation, exportation, transportation, storage, packaging, blending, distribution or sale of biofuels, commits an offence  and  shall, upon  conviction,  be  liable to a fine as prescribed under Section 221 of the Act.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CALL FOR COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT ENERGY (RENEWABLE ENERGY RESOURCE) REGULATIONS, 2025 AND DRAFT REGULATORY IMPACT STATEMENT
texts.text: CALL FOR COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT ENERGY (RENEWABLE ENERGY RESOURCE) REGULATIONS, 2025 AND DRAFT REGULATORY IMPACT STATEMENT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (the Authority) is Kenya's energy sector regulatory agency established under the Energy Act, 2019, and is responsible for the technical and economic regulation of  the  Electricity,  Renewable  Energy  and  Petroleum  subsectors  in Kenya.  The  Authority  has  developed  the  Draft  Energy  (Renewable Energy Resource) Regulations, 2025 together with the associated Draft Regulatory  Impact  Statement.  The  purpose  of  the  regulations  is  to promote investment in renewable energy by providing a framework for permitting exploration of renewable energy resources and licensing of geothermal exploitation.
texts.text: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (the Authority) is Kenya's energy sector regulatory agency established under the Energy Act, 2019, and is responsible for the technical and economic regulation of  the  Electricity,  Renewable  Energy  and  Petroleum  subsectors  in Kenya.  The  Authority  has  developed  the  Draft  Energy  (Renewable Energy Resource) Regulations, 2025 together with the associated Draft Regulatory  Impact  Statement.  The  purpose  of  the  regulations  is  to promote investment in renewable energy by providing a framework for permitting exploration of renewable energy resources and licensing of geothermal exploitation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Stakeholders are requested to review the aforementioned documents and provide feedback that will be considered for incorporation in the regulations that will be Gazetted.
texts.text: Stakeholders are requested to review the aforementioned documents and provide feedback that will be considered for incorporation in the regulations that will be Gazetted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Your written  comments on the Draft Energy (Renewable Energy Resource) Regulations, 2025 and the draft Regulatory Impact Statement using the prescribed public comments form on the Authority's  website,  may  be  submitted  to  reach  the  undersigned  not later than thirty (30) days from the date of publication of this notice. The memoranda  marked  'PUBLIC  COMMENTS  ON  DRAFT RENEWABLE  ENERGY  RESOURCE  REGULATIONS'  can  be hand delivered to the Authority offices or sent by email to renewableenergy@epra.go.ke.

The memoranda  marked  'PUBLIC  COMMENTS  ON  DRAFT RENEWABLE  ENERGY  RESOURCE  REGULATIONS'  can  be hand delivered to the Authority offices or sent by email to renewableenergy@epra.go.ke.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This Geothermal Resources Licence is granted this …………. day of …………..…..20YY  by  the  Government  of  the  Republic  of  Kenya (hereafter  referred  to  as  the  'Government'  )  represented  for  the purpose of this Licence by the Cabinet Secretary in charge of Energy and  Petroleum  (hereafter  referred  to  as  the  'Cabinet  Secretary')  of P.O. Box 30582- 00100, Nairobi to ………………………. , a limited liability company incorporated under the laws of Kenya and having a registered place of Business at  …………………………….. (hereafter referred to as the 'Licensee' which expression includes its successors) pursuant to the Energy  Act,  2019  (the 'Act') and the  Energy (Renewable Energy Resource) Regulations, 2024 (the 'Regulations').
texts.text: This Geothermal Resources Licence is granted this …………. day of …………..…..20YY  by  the  Government  of  the  Republic  of  Kenya (hereafter  referred  to  as  the  'Government'  )  represented  for  the purpose of this Licence by the Cabinet Secretary in charge of Energy and  Petroleum  (hereafter  referred  to  as  the  'Cabinet  Secretary')  of P.O. Box 30582- 00100, Nairobi to ………………………. , a limited liability company incorporated under the laws of Kenya and having a registered place of Business at  …………………………….. (hereafter referred to as the 'Licensee' which expression includes its successors) pursuant to the Energy  Act,  2019  (the 'Act') and the  Energy (Renewable Energy Resource) Regulations, 2024 (the 'Regulations').

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. The Licensee is hereby granted the following exclusive rights:
texts.text: The Licensee is hereby granted the following exclusive rights:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) The  right  and  privilege  to  enter  and  explore,  drill  for, extract,  produce,  utilize  and  dispose  of  geothermal  steam and  associated  geothermal  resources  in  or  under  the  land described in Appendix I and shown on the map set forth in Appendix II hereof (the 'Licence Area').
texts.text: (a) The  right  and  privilege  to  enter  and  explore,  drill  for, extract,  produce,  utilize  and  dispose  of  geothermal  steam and  associated  geothermal  resources  in  or  under  the  land described in Appendix I and shown on the map set forth in Appendix II hereof (the 'Licence Area').

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) The  right to construct or erect and  use, operate  and maintain within the Licence Area, together with ingress and egress there upon all wells, pumps, pipes, pipelines, buildings, plants, sumps, brine pits, reservoirs, tanks, waterworks, pumping stations, roads, electric power generating  plants,  transmission  lines,  industrial  facilities; electric,  telegraph  or  telephone  lines  or  cables  and  such other  works  and  structures  and  to  use  so  much  of  the surface  of  the  land  within  the  Licence  Area  as  may  be necessary or convenient for the production, utilization and processing of geothermal resources or for the full enjoyment  of  the  rights granted hereunder,  subject to compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.
texts.text: (b) The  right to construct or erect and  use, operate  and maintain within the Licence Area, together with ingress and egress there upon all wells, pumps, pipes, pipelines, buildings, plants, sumps, brine pits, reservoirs, tanks, waterworks, pumping stations, roads, electric power generating  plants,  transmission  lines,  industrial  facilities; electric,  telegraph  or  telephone  lines  or  cables  and  such other  works  and  structures  and  to  use  so  much  of  the surface  of  the  land  within  the  Licence  Area  as  may  be necessary or convenient for the production, utilization and processing of geothermal resources or for the full enjoyment  of  the  rights granted hereunder,  subject to compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) In so far as it may be necessary for and in connection with the operations described in this Licence, the right to:
texts.text: (c) In so far as it may be necessary for and in connection with the operations described in this Licence, the right to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) drill and construct all necessary boreholes;
texts.text: (i) drill and construct all necessary boreholes;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) erect, construct and maintain houses and buildings for the Licensee's own use and for use by the Licensee's employees;
texts.text: (ii) erect, construct and maintain houses and buildings for the Licensee's own use and for use by the Licensee's employees;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) erect,  construct  and  maintain  plant,  machinery, buildings and other erections as may be necessary;
texts.text: (iii) erect,  construct  and  maintain  plant,  machinery, buildings and other erections as may be necessary;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) subject  to  the  Water  Act  2016,  reclaim  and utilize any water; and
texts.text: (v) subject  to  the  Water  Act  2016,  reclaim  and utilize any water; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (vi) construct and maintain roads and other means of communication and convenience;
texts.text: (vi) construct and maintain roads and other means of communication and convenience;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: subject to compliance with the Environment Management and Co-ordination Act, 2015, the Energy Act, 2019 and all other applicable laws and regulations
texts.text: subject to compliance with the Environment Management and Co-ordination Act, 2015, the Energy Act, 2019 and all other applicable laws and regulations

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) The  rights  to take,  use,  sell  or  apply  the  geothermal resources  for  the  purpose  of  generating  electric  power,  at any power station(s) to be erected upon the Licence Area, under  Power  Purchase  Agreement(s)  to  be  made  between the  Licensee,  and/or  an  affiliate  of  the  Licensee  approved by the Authority, and the  off taker. For the purposes of this sub-clause 1(d), 'affiliate' shall mean an entity that directly or  indirectly  owns,  is  owned  by  or  is  under  common ownership with the Licensee, and as used in this definition, 'own' shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of any equity interests in or voting rights in such entity' 'Off taker'  shall  mean  any  entity  authorized  to  enter  into  a power purchase agreement with the Licensee.
texts.text: (d) The  rights  to take,  use,  sell  or  apply  the  geothermal resources  for  the  purpose  of  generating  electric  power,  at any power station(s) to be erected upon the Licence Area, under  Power  Purchase  Agreement(s)  to  be  made  between the  Licensee,  and/or  an  affiliate  of  the  Licensee  approved by the Authority, and the  off taker. For the purposes of this sub-clause 1(d), 'affiliate' shall mean an entity that directly or  indirectly  owns,  is  owned  by  or  is  under  common ownership with the Licensee, and as used in this definition, 'own' shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of any equity interests in or voting rights in such entity' 'Off taker'  shall  mean  any  entity  authorized  to  enter  into  a power purchase agreement with the Licensee.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) The  rights  to take,  use,  sell  or  apply  the  geothermal resources for the purpose of direct uses, at any facilities to be erected upon the Licence Area
texts.text: (e) The  rights  to take,  use,  sell  or  apply  the  geothermal resources for the purpose of direct uses, at any facilities to be erected upon the Licence Area

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. The rights granted shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the date hereof and such term may be renewed at the option of the Licensee,  for  one  further  period  of  five  (5)  years;  provided  the licensee has complied with all the terms hereof; and provided that the  Cabinet  Secretary  shall  in  granting  the  Licence,  allow  an exploration phase of a period not exceeding three years and if at the  end  of  that  period  no  geothermal  resource  of  potential commercial  interest  is  discovered  the  Cabinet  Secretary  may require the Licensee to surrender the Licence Area.
texts.text: The rights granted shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the date hereof and such term may be renewed at the option of the Licensee,  for  one  further  period  of  five  (5)  years;  provided  the licensee has complied with all the terms hereof; and provided that the  Cabinet  Secretary  shall  in  granting  the  Licence,  allow  an exploration phase of a period not exceeding three years and if at the  end  of  that  period  no  geothermal  resource  of  potential commercial  interest  is  discovered  the  Cabinet  Secretary  may require the Licensee to surrender the Licence Area.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) Conduct  geothermal  surface  exploration  in  the  Licence Area for a period of three (3) years commencing the date of issuance of this Licence.
texts.text: (a) Conduct  geothermal  surface  exploration  in  the  Licence Area for a period of three (3) years commencing the date of issuance of this Licence.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) Carry out development of the power station(s) or direct use facilities  in  a  period  of  three  (3)  years,  which  includes construction, and commissioning, starting from the end of the three (3) years geothermal exploration period under (a).
texts.text: (b) Carry out development of the power station(s) or direct use facilities  in  a  period  of  three  (3)  years,  which  includes construction, and commissioning, starting from the end of the three (3) years geothermal exploration period under (a).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) Mount  the  first  development  plan  to  target  generating capacity  of  ……..  MW  of  electrical  power  or  thermal energy of …….MW (thermal) or the maximum economically available as defined by a Board of Consultants hired by the Cabinet Secretary through competitive sourcing as per applicable law, under Terms of Reference (ToRs) proposed by the Licensee and approved
texts.text: (c) Mount  the  first  development  plan  to  target  generating capacity  of  ……..  MW  of  electrical  power  or  thermal energy of …….MW (thermal) or the maximum economically available as defined by a Board of Consultants hired by the Cabinet Secretary through competitive sourcing as per applicable law, under Terms of Reference (ToRs) proposed by the Licensee and approved

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: by  the  Cabinet  Secretary,  and  paid  for  by  the  Licensee, before the end of the three (3) years geothermal exploration period under (a).
texts.text: by  the  Cabinet  Secretary,  and  paid  for  by  the  Licensee, before the end of the three (3) years geothermal exploration period under (a).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) Bear the costs of any consultancy relating to the project in full.
texts.text: (d) Bear the costs of any consultancy relating to the project in full.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) Deposit  the  full  amount  of  the  consultancy  fee  for  the Board of Consultants with the Cabinet Secretary on completion of the tendering process.
texts.text: (e) Deposit  the  full  amount  of  the  consultancy  fee  for  the Board of Consultants with the Cabinet Secretary on completion of the tendering process.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) Also  deposit  with  the  Cabinet  Secretary  the  full  amount required by the Survey of Kenya  for preparation of Cadastral  Maps  and  other  supporting  documents  of  the Licence Area which will be needed for registration of rights to the land.
texts.text: (f) Also  deposit  with  the  Cabinet  Secretary  the  full  amount required by the Survey of Kenya  for preparation of Cadastral  Maps  and  other  supporting  documents  of  the Licence Area which will be needed for registration of rights to the land.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. The Licensee  shall  provide  the  Cabinet  Secretary  with  periodic written reports of the progress of operations under this licence as set forth in Appendix III.
texts.text: The Licensee  shall  provide  the  Cabinet  Secretary  with  periodic written reports of the progress of operations under this licence as set forth in Appendix III.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Where the Licensee during the exploration phase, discovers geothermal  resources  which  is  of  potential  commercial interest,  they  shall  within  sixty  (60)  days  after  discovery submit  an  appraisal  programme  to  the  Cabinet  Secretary for approval.
texts.text: Where the Licensee during the exploration phase, discovers geothermal  resources  which  is  of  potential  commercial interest,  they  shall  within  sixty  (60)  days  after  discovery submit  an  appraisal  programme  to  the  Cabinet  Secretary for approval.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) If the appraisal programme results into a declaration by the Cabinet Secretary and the Licensee of a viable commercial geothermal  resource,  the  Licensee  shall,  within  twelve (12)  months  from  the  date  of  declaration,  submit  to  the Cabinet Secretary a development and production programme which shall be in the form of Appendix IV.

Upon  cessation  of  the  force  majeure  event,  the party  no  longer  affected  shall  promptly  notify  the  other party.

texts.text: (c) For the purpose of this clause promptly shall be deemed to mean a period of twenty-one (21) days.

texts.orig: (d) Where the party not affected disputes the existence of force majeure,  that  dispute  shall  be  referred  to  arbitration  in accordance with the provisions for arbitration contained in this Licence.
texts.text: (d) Where the party not affected disputes the existence of force majeure,  that  dispute  shall  be  referred  to  arbitration  in accordance with the provisions for arbitration contained in this Licence.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (e) Where  an  obligation  is  suspended  by  force  majeure  for more than one (1) year, the parties may agree to terminate this Licence by notice in writing without further obligations; provided that each party shall remain liable for the fulfillment of any obligation which remains unperformed at that time.
texts.text: (e) Where  an  obligation  is  suspended  by  force  majeure  for more than one (1) year, the parties may agree to terminate this Licence by notice in writing without further obligations; provided that each party shall remain liable for the fulfillment of any obligation which remains unperformed at that time.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) Subject  to  paragraph  (e)  of  this  clause,  the  term  of  the Licence  shall  be  automatically  extended  for  the  period  of the force majeure.
texts.text: (f) Subject  to  paragraph  (e)  of  this  clause,  the  term  of  the Licence  shall  be  automatically  extended  for  the  period  of the force majeure.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) Whenever any dispute arises between the Licensee and an owner of private land or community land, then the owner of the private land or community land shall participate in the dispute resolution as stakeholder.
texts.text: (a) Whenever any dispute arises between the Licensee and an owner of private land or community land, then the owner of the private land or community land shall participate in the dispute resolution as stakeholder.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (b) Except as otherwise provided in this Licence, any question or dispute arising out of or in relation to or in connection with  this Licence  shall,  as  far as possible,  be  settled amicably.    Where  no  settlement  is  reached  within  thirty (30) days from the date of the dispute, such dispute shall be referred  to  arbitration  in  accordance  with  the  provisions hereinafter contained.
texts.text: (b) Except as otherwise provided in this Licence, any question or dispute arising out of or in relation to or in connection with  this Licence  shall,  as  far as possible,  be  settled amicably.    Where  no  settlement  is  reached  within  thirty (30) days from the date of the dispute, such dispute shall be referred  to  arbitration  in  accordance  with  the  provisions hereinafter contained.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (c) All  disputes  arising  out  of  this  License  or  relating  to  any investment made under it for settlement by arbitration shall be  settled  in  Kenya  in  accordance  with  Arbitration  Act, 1995(Revised, 2012) of the Laws of Kenya.
texts.text: (c) All  disputes  arising  out  of  this  License  or  relating  to  any investment made under it for settlement by arbitration shall be  settled  in  Kenya  in  accordance  with  Arbitration  Act, 1995(Revised, 2012) of the Laws of Kenya.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (d) Any  such  arbitration  proceeding  shall  be  conducted  in accordance  with  the  Rules  of  Procedure  for  Arbitration proceedings  in  effect  in  Kenya  on  the  date  on  which  the proceeding is instituted.
texts.text: (d) Any  such  arbitration  proceeding  shall  be  conducted  in accordance  with  the  Rules  of  Procedure  for  Arbitration proceedings  in  effect  in  Kenya  on  the  date  on  which  the proceeding is instituted.

(1) An application for exploration permit shall be made electronically or in any other manner that the Cabinet Secretary may, from time to time, prescribe and shall be in the form set out in First Schedule and accompanied by proof of payment of the application fees set out in the Second Schedule.
(1) Any objections to the grant of a permit as a result of the public notice issued under Regulation 6(2) shall be submitted not later than thirty days after the date of the public notice.
(2) Where the Cabinet Secretary receives an objection to the grant of a permit, he shall request the applicant to address, in writing, the issues raised in the objection within fifteen days.

(3) The Cabinet Secretary may hear any objections in public, at a time and place of which not less than fifteen days' notice shall be given to the applicant and to every objector and the Cabinet Secretary shall make known their decision regarding any objection within thirty days after the hearing.

The Cabinet Secretary shall, in granting or rejecting an application for an exploration permit, consider-
(ii) output of consultation with the relevant county government, local communities or any other relevant government agency;
(iii) the impact of the undertaking on the social, cultural or recreational life of the community;
land use or the location of the undertaking;
(v) economic and financial benefits to the country or area of supply of the undertaking;
the economic and energy policies in place at the time;
(vii) that the contractual rights, privileges, liabilities and obligations accrued to an existing licensee or any other person are not materially adversely affected;
(viii) the cost of the undertaking and financing arrangements;

the ability of the applicant to operate in a manner designed to protect the health and safety of its employees and users of the service for which the permit is required and other members of the public who would be affected by the undertaking;
the technical and financial capacity of the applicant to undertake the exploration for which the authority is required;
any representations or objections in regulation 7(1); and
any other matter that the Cabinet Secretary may consider likely to have a bearing on the undertaking.

table_cells.text: Where the Cabinet Secretary approves the application, the approval shall grant the applicant site exclusivity for the period of the permit.

A holder of an exploration permit shall within sixty (60) days of expiry of the permit or at the end of the exploration submit an exploration report to the Cabinet Secretary. The report shall include an appraisal of the resource in the resource area.
A holder of the permit shall have the first right of exploitation of a feasible resource.
A holder of a permit under these regulations, who intends to utilize the resource shall obtain the applicable approvals.
Where the resource in sub regulation (7) is geothermal, the holder of the permit shall obtain a geothermal resource license.
